front: Play: Sir Epicure Mammon seeks the philosopher's stone; Dapper meets 'Queen of Fairy' (Dol Common in disguise); scams run by Subtle, Face, and Dol Common
Play: Venetian man feigns illness to receive gifts from legacy hunters Corvino, Voltore, and Corbaccio; servant Mosca assists
Play: puppets lift their clothes to refute Zeal-of-the-Land Busy's cross-dressing accusations; Justice Overdo goes undercover
Poem: 'Drink to me only with thine eyes'; speaker sends a rosy wreath that now 'smells not of itself, but thee'
Poem: mourns a child who died at seven, called 'his best piece of poetry' and 'thou child of my right hand'
Poem: early country house poem praising Philip Sidney's estate as 'not built to envious show'
Playwright: eulogized Shakespeare as 'not of an age, but for all time' in the First Folio preface
Playwright: feuded with Marston and Dekker in the 'War of the Theatres'; killed Gabriel Spenser in a duel
Playwright: Cavalier poets called themselves the 'Tribe of Ben' or 'Sons of Ben'; Hawthornden Manuscripts record conversations with Drummond
